prestazioni: Replace the stock S&S X-Wedge V-twin (engine code: XWE-1983cc) with a fully built S&S Cycle 2,200cc stroker kit (XWE-2200), featuring forged 4.125" pistons, billet steel connecting rods, and a billet crankshaft with 4.5" stroke. Pair with a S&S Super E carburetor (58mm bore) and a custom 3-into-1 stainless steel exhaust system by Black Widow Performance, incorporating 2.5" mandrel-bent tubing and a Burns Stainless muffler for optimized scavenging.

Install a custom billet aluminum flywheel (10.5 lbs) from Barnett Tool & Engineering, mated to a Barnett Scorpion multi-plate clutch (3 friction plates) with a billet steel pressure plate. Upgrade the primary drive to a Gates Carbon Drive CDX belt system (14mm width) for reduced rotational mass and improved throttle response.

Fabricate a custom aluminum radiator by Griffin Thermal Products (2-row, 1.25" tubes) with dual 12" SPAL puller fans (1,250 CFM each) and a Davies Craig EWP115 electric water pump. Integrate a custom oil cooler (Setrab 19-row, 5.5" x 11") with AN-10 braided lines and a Mocal remote oil filter mount to address the X-Wedge’s inherent cooling limitations.
estetica: Replace the stock cycle fenders with hand-formed aluminum panels by Brooklands Speedwell, featuring a brushed finish and riveted leather straps (vintage aviation-style). Install a custom aluminum nosecone by RS Motorsports, incorporating a retro-styled mesh grille and integrated LED turn signals (Luxxen LX-30).

Fit a custom leather-wrapped cockpit by Royal Steering Wheels, featuring a 14" Moto-Lita aluminum-spoke wheel with a quick-release hub. Replace the stock bench seat with a pair of Recaro SRD bucket seats (carbon-Kevlar shell) mounted on custom aluminum sliders by Corbeau, upholstered in Connolly Bridge of Weir leather with contrasting stitching.

Apply a custom paint scheme by RS Coachworks, using a base of Aston Martin Racing Green (PPG DBC-9907) with hand-painted gold leaf pinstriping (1/16" width) and a clear-coated satin finish. Add period-correct decals by Classic Car Decals, including Morgan’s 1930s Brooklands race livery and a custom "XWE-2200" engine displacement badge on the fuel tank.
